sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip INET, value DOUBLE PRECISION, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW());
SELECT id, atan(value) as arc_tangent, ip FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND ip <<= inet '192.168.1.0/24';
SELECT id, atan(value) AS arc_tangent FROM test_data WHERE atan(value) IS NOT NULL GROUP BY arc_tangent HAVING arc_tangent > 0;
SELECT ip, COUNT(*) as occurrences FROM test_data GROUP BY ip HAVING inet '192.168.1.0/24' <<= ip AND COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, ip, value FROM test_data WHERE value > (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) * atan(1) ORDER BY value;
CREATE TABLE other_data (data_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, reference_ip INET, check_value DOUBLE PRECISION);
INSERT INTO other_data (reference_ip, check_value) VALUES (inet '192.168.1.5', atan(0.5)), (inet '192.168.2.5', atan(-1.5));
SELECT d.id, d.ip, d.value, o.check_value FROM test_data d INNER JOIN other_data o ON o.reference_ip <<= d.ip WHERE d.value > 0;
SELECT id, atan(value) as arc_tangent FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL) GROUP BY arc_tangent ORDER BY arc_tangent DESC;
SELECT value, COUNT(id) as count FROM test_data GROUP BY value HAVING count > 1 AND value <<= (SELECT MIN(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) + 1;
